papers.title_en: Stabilising Evaporating Soap Films with Salt
papers.authors: Victor Ziapkoff, François Boulogne, Anniina Salonen, Emmanuelle Rio
papers.evidence: 垂直皂膜厚度测量、蒸发及盐稳定化
papers.tldr: 在蒸发条件下，垂直皂膜（泡沫膜）因排水和蒸发而迅速变薄，其稳定性受湿度影响显著。本研究系统考察了高浓度NaCl（32.5 g/L）对TTAB皂膜寿命和厚度的影响，实验在可控湿度下进行。结果发现，盐的存在使膜寿命在所有湿度下均提高；且两者变薄动力学在100 nm前几乎相同，随后无盐膜在约10 nm破裂，而含盐膜形成约5 nm的牛顿黑膜并长期稳定。分析表明，蒸发率在膜厚低于400 nm时变得与排水率相当，但盐的主要作用是促进超薄黑膜形成。这项工作阐明了盐稳定蒸发皂膜的关键机制，为泡沫、乳液的长期稳定提供了新思路。
papers.abstract_en: We investigate the effect of a high concentration (32.5 g.L$^{-1}$) of sodium chloride (NaCl) on TTAB (tetradecyltrimethylammonium bromide) vertical soap films also called foam films, pulled out of a bath under controlled humidity conditions. We observe that the film lifetime increases with relative humidity, both in the presence and absence of salt. At any given humidity, the presence of NaCl systematically enhances film stability. Our film thickness measurements show that the thinning dynamics with or without salt are nearly identical down to 100 nm. Down to that thickness, the effect of evaporation can be rationalised by a constant evaporation rate, which becomes non-negligible compared to the drainage rate at film thicknesses below 400 nm. The main effect of salt is the stabilisation of a Newton black film at a thickness of approximately 5~nm, whereas in the absence of salt, the film ruptures upon reaching a critical thickness of about 10 nm.
